Class DBManager

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am25.server.model.DBmanager.DBManager
public class DBManager extends Object
Manages all interactions with the remote AWS RDS leaderboard database. Maintains a single persistent Connection that is lazily opened on first use and reused for all subsequent operations. Credentials are loaded from a local DBcred file in the resources directory, formatted as KEY=VALUE pairs (DB_USER and DB_PASSWORD).

    Constructor
    Description

    Default constructor.

    Closes the active database connection if one is open.

    Returns the active database connection, opening a new one if needed.

    Retrieves the all-time leaderboard for games played with the given number of players, ordered by cumulative score descending.

    Returns the 1-based leaderboard position of the given player for games played with the specified number of players.

  • Constructor Details

    • DBManager

      public DBManager()
      Default constructor. An instance is not required to use this class since all methods are static.

  • Method Details

    • getConnection

      public static Connection getConnection() throws SQLException, IOException
      Returns the active database connection, opening a new one if needed.
      Returns:
      an open Connection to the leaderboard database.
      Throws:
      SQLException - if the JDBC driver cannot establish the connection.
      IOException - if the DBcred credentials file cannot be read.

    • closeConnection

      public static void closeConnection() throws SQLException
      Closes the active database connection if one is open. Should be called only at server shutdown.
      Throws:
      SQLException - if closing the connection fails.

    • logMatch

      public static void logMatch(List<Player> players) throws SQLException, IOException
      Persists the result of a completed match to the database. Inserts a new row in the game table, registers any unknown players in the player table, and records each player's score in the result table. The list must contain all players ordered by final ranking (1st to last), so that the correct score table is applied via UtilitiesFunction.getScore(int, int).
      Parameters:
      players - all players sorted by finishing position (best first).
      Throws:
      SQLException - if any database operation fails.
      IOException - if the credentials file cannot be read.

    • getLeaderboard

      public static List<String> getLeaderboard(int playerCount) throws SQLException, IOException
      Retrieves the all-time leaderboard for games played with the given number of players, ordered by cumulative score descending. Each entry is formatted as "N. nickname - totalScore".
      Parameters:
      playerCount - the number of players per game to filter by (2–5).
      Returns:
      an ordered list of leaderboard entries, empty if no data exists.
      Throws:
      SQLException - if the query fails.
      IOException - if the credentials file cannot be read.

    • getPlayerPosition

      public static int getPlayerPosition(String nickname, int playerCount) throws SQLException, IOException
      Returns the 1-based leaderboard position of the given player for games played with the specified number of players.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the player's nickname to look up.
      playerCount - the number of players per game to filter by (2–5).
      Returns:
      the player's rank (1 = first), or -1 if not found.
      Throws:
      SQLException - if the query fails.
      IOException - if the credentials file cannot be read.
